conbody.p: Red text in the terminal is not a catastrophe. Error messages are informational — they tell you exactly what went wrong and where. Learning to read them is the most valuable debugging skill you can develop.

section.title: Anatomy of a Python Error Message
section.p: A Python error message has three key parts:

dlentry.dd: The sequence of file and line references leading to the error. Read it from bottom to top — the bottom line is where the error actually occurred.
The category of error, for example ModuleNotFoundError or SyntaxError. The type alone often tells you what went wrong.
A human-readable description of the specific problem, for example No module named 'spacy'.

example.title: Example: ModuleNotFoundError
example.p: This error means Python cannot find the spaCy library. The most common cause is that your virtual environment is not activated. Run conda activate nlp-course and try again.
